name: Rip Taylor
blurb: The Crying Comedian who turned a bad set into a sixty-year career — moustache, toupee, confetti by the bucket, and a voice pitched to the back of the room whether the room held twelve people or twelve hundred.
bio: Rip Taylor's whole act is a punchline about itself: in the early 1960s he was dying on The Merv Griffin Show, tore his cue cards into pieces in frustration, threw the scraps over his head, and accidentally invented the gag that paid his rent until 2019. After that he was a force of nature designed for talk-show couches and Vegas — loud, broad, bewigged, perpetually one note from a confetti drop. He warmed up rooms for Sinatra, Sammy Davis Jr., Judy Garland, and Ann-Margret, made dozens of Tonight Show appearances with Carson, hosted Chuck Barris's deliberately tacky The $1.98 Beauty Show (1978–80), and remained a celebrity-square fixture on Hollywood Squares, Match Game, and The Gong Show. As stand-up he was a prop comic before the term was fashionable; as a TV personality he was one of the great pure presences of the era.

timeline.ev: Born Charles Elmer Taylor Jr., January 13, in Washington, D.C.
The Merv Griffin Show confetti accident invents the trademark gag
Begins his Tonight Show run with Johnny Carson
Hosts The $1.98 Beauty Show (through 1980), Chuck Barris's deliberate trash classic
Begins regular voice work — DuckTales, Tom and Jerry Kids, eventually Wayne's World 2 (1993)
Premieres his autobiographical one-man show It Ain't All Confetti
Dies October 6 in Beverly Hills, age 88

accolades: Host of The $1.98 Beauty Show (1978–1980), syndicated cult classic
Las Vegas opener for Sinatra, Sammy Davis Jr., Judy Garland, Ann-Margret, Debbie Reynolds
Dozens of appearances on The Tonight Show Starring Johnny Carson and The Merv Griffin Show
Hollywood Squares, Match Game, To Tell the Truth, The Gong Show panel regular
